verdict.headline: DETECTOR HAS TIMING SKILL, BUT THE BOOK DOES NOT DEPLOY: the bear DETECTOR genuinely concentrates its ON-time in the true bear window (stitched precision 0.36 vs base-rate 0.12, recall 0.74, beats shuffle-95 in 0.9 of seeds; the GATED book beats the equal-freq SHUFFLE on net at every TF) -- so this is NOT the dynamic-engine null, the detector WORKS. BUT the gated longshort BOOK still loses ~0.3-2.0pp net vs trend-alone on the full cycle and does not reduce DD. MECHANISM (per-segment decomposition, verified): the conditional BEAR gain is TINY (~+0.1pp -- the synthetic bear is only ~12% of the cycle and mild in NET terms), while the gate's residual FALSE-ALARM firings in the bull/chop segments (~18-22% on-time even there, each shorting a RISING market) plus borrow sum to ~-1.6pp. On a bull-dominated cycle, even a precise gate's small false-alarm rate costs more than the short bear earns. Honest answer: TREND-ALONE+trail is the deployable long-only book; the detector is real but binary longshort insurance is -EV here. The open door (untestable on 2020): a DEEPER/LONGER bear (e.g. 2022 grind-down) where the bear is a larger NET share of the cycle -- the detector machinery is built + validated to test it the moment such a band is in scope.
lines: HEADLINE: DETECTOR HAS TIMING SKILL, BUT THE BOOK DOES NOT DEPLOY: the bear DETECTOR genuinely concentrates its ON-time in the true bear window (stitched precision 0.36 vs base-rate 0.12, recall 0.74, beats shuffle-95 in 0.9 of seeds; the GATED book beats the equal-freq SHUFFLE on net at every TF) -- so this is NOT the dynamic-engine null, the detector WORKS. BUT the gated longshort BOOK still loses ~0.3-2.0pp net vs trend-alone on the full cycle and does not reduce DD. MECHANISM (per-segment decomposition, verified): the conditional BEAR gain is TINY (~+0.1pp -- the synthetic bear is only ~12% of the cycle and mild in NET terms), while the gate's residual FALSE-ALARM firings in the bull/chop segments (~18-22% on-time even there, each shorting a RISING market) plus borrow sum to ~-1.6pp. On a bull-dominated cycle, even a precise gate's small false-alarm rate costs more than the short bear earns. Honest answer: TREND-ALONE+trail is the deployable long-only book; the detector is real but binary longshort insurance is -EV here. The open door (untestable on 2020): a DEEPER/LONGER bear (e.g. 2022 grind-down) where the bear is a larger NET share of the cycle -- the detector machinery is built + validated to test it the moment such a band is in scope.
DETECTOR (pre-registered on 2020 TRAIN, FROZEN): close < SMA(20), arm after 5 bars, disarm after 3 bars (causal, 1-bar-shifted).
Q0 (THE SKILL TEST, decisive): on the STITCHED full-cycle path -- where regime TRANSITIONS exist --
does the detector concentrate its ON-time in the TRUE bear window better than a random toggle?
detector precision 0.36 (on-day is true-bear) vs base-rate 0.12 and recall 0.74; precision-minus-shuffle 0.24pp; beats shuffle-95 in 0.9 of seeds -> DETECTOR HAS REAL TIMING SKILL
(NOTE: standalone single-regime panels are too short for a slow gate to warm up -- the gate is
~always-off there, so the standalone shuffle is DEGENERATE; the stitched path is the proper test.)
Q1 (THE SYNTHESIS, stitched full-cycle path): does GATING capture MOST of the always-on's bear
DD-protection while paying LITTLE of its full-cycle net-drag? (CAPTURED = gated's stitched
DD-protection / always-on's; PAID = gated's stitched net-drag / always-on's. Ideal=high cap, low drag.)
1d   : bear-protection CAPTURED -64.9% (gated -0.72pp DD vs always-on 1.11pp); full-cycle drag PAID 14.4% (gated -2.03pp vs always-on -14.1pp) -> no clean capture/drag separation
4h   : bear-protection CAPTURED -15.5% (gated -0.13pp DD vs always-on 0.84pp); full-cycle drag PAID 10.6% (gated -0.53pp vs always-on -5.0pp) -> no clean capture/drag separation
2h   : bear-protection CAPTURED -20.3% (gated -0.16pp DD vs always-on 0.79pp); full-cycle drag PAID 10.3% (gated -0.52pp vs always-on -5.03pp) -> no clean capture/drag separation
1h   : bear-protection CAPTURED -11.2% (gated -0.11pp DD vs always-on 0.98pp); full-cycle drag PAID 5.6% (gated -0.33pp vs always-on -5.9pp) -> no clean capture/drag separation
30m  : bear-protection CAPTURED -6.5% (gated -0.06pp DD vs always-on 0.93pp); full-cycle drag PAID 7.2% (gated -0.43pp vs always-on -5.98pp) -> no clean capture/drag separation
15m  : bear-protection CAPTURED -6.5% (gated -0.06pp DD vs always-on 0.93pp); full-cycle drag PAID 7.2% (gated -0.43pp vs always-on -5.98pp) -> no clean capture/drag separation
Q2 (CONTROL b, stitched path): does the GATED book beat TREND-ALONE on net AND maxDD?
(PHASE 6's STATIC always-on book FAILED this -- the conditional book must clear trend to be worth it)
1d   : vs trend -- net -2.03pp (beats=False), maxDD -0.71pp (beats=False), worst-seed net -3.3pp -> does NOT clear trend
4h   : vs trend -- net -0.53pp (beats=False), maxDD -0.13pp (beats=False), worst-seed net -2.4pp -> does NOT clear trend
2h   : vs trend -- net -0.52pp (beats=False), maxDD -0.16pp (beats=False), worst-seed net -2.4pp -> does NOT clear trend
1h   : vs trend -- net -0.33pp (beats=False), maxDD -0.11pp (beats=False), worst-seed net -1.8pp -> does NOT clear trend
30m  : vs trend -- net -0.44pp (beats=False), maxDD -0.06pp (beats=False), worst-seed net -3.2pp -> does NOT clear trend
15m  : vs trend -- net -0.44pp (beats=False), maxDD -0.06pp (beats=False), worst-seed net -3.2pp -> does NOT clear trend
Q3 (CONTROL c, MANDATORY -- the skill test, stitched path + gate-precision): does the DETECTOR's
TIMING beat a RANDOM-TIMED toggle of EQUAL on-frequency? (the shuffle that killed the dynamic
engine. Both books carry the SAME longshort exposure; only the timing differs.)
1d   : vs shuffle -- net +1.43pp (beats=True), maxDD -0.71pp (beats=False) -> detector TIMING adds value over random
4h   : vs shuffle -- net +0.76pp (beats=True), maxDD -0.23pp (beats=False) -> detector TIMING adds value over random
2h   : vs shuffle -- net +0.8pp (beats=True), maxDD -0.24pp (beats=False) -> detector TIMING adds value over random
1h   : vs shuffle -- net +1.08pp (beats=True), maxDD -0.25pp (beats=False) -> detector TIMING adds value over random
30m  : vs shuffle -- net +1.0pp (beats=True), maxDD -0.18pp (beats=False) -> detector TIMING adds value over random
15m  : vs shuffle -- net +1.0pp (beats=True), maxDD -0.18pp (beats=False) -> detector TIMING adds value over random
(gate-precision skill, stitched: precision 0.36 vs base-rate 0.12, beats shuffle-95 in 0.9 of seeds -> the gate's ON-time IS concentrated in the true bear, even if the BOOK payoff is a wash.)
Q3b (MECHANISM, why a SKILLED detector still does not deploy): per-segment gated-minus-trend (1d):
bull0    (bull ): gated-minus-trend -0.53pp | gate on-frac 0.18
bear1    (bear ): gated-minus-trend +0.11pp | gate on-frac 0.74
chop2    (chop ): gated-minus-trend -0.41pp | gate on-frac 0.18
bull3    (bull ): gated-minus-trend -0.71pp | gate on-frac 0.22
-> the conditional BEAR gain is TINY; the gate's residual FALSE-ALARM firings in bull/chop (short into a rising market) + borrow OUTWEIGH it. A precise gate still loses on a bull-dominated cycle.
Q4: 2020-REAL OOS (Oct-Dec BULL) anchor -- on a real bull the gate SHOULD be ~off (gated ~= trend):
1d   : TREND net 13.6% (DD -11.0) vs GATED net 12.3% (DD -11.2); OOS gate on-frac 0.25 (near 0 = correctly OFF in the bull)
4h   : TREND net 19.3% (DD -6.2) vs GATED net 17.9% (DD -6.5); OOS gate on-frac 0.152 (near 0 = correctly OFF in the bull)
2h   : TREND net 34.6% (DD -5.6) vs GATED net 31.7% (DD -6.2); OOS gate on-frac 0.12 (near 0 = correctly OFF in the bull)
1h   : TREND net 50.4% (DD -5.8) vs GATED net 47.6% (DD -6.0); OOS gate on-frac 0.12 (near 0 = correctly OFF in the bull)
30m  : TREND net 46.6% (DD -7.9) vs GATED net 43.0% (DD -7.9); OOS gate on-frac 0.076 (near 0 = correctly OFF in the bull)
15m  : TREND net 54.6% (DD -11.5) vs GATED net 47.9% (DD -11.5); OOS gate on-frac 0.087 (near 0 = correctly OFF in the bull)

CAVEATS (binding): (1) SYNTHETIC stress surface from PHASE 3/6's VALIDATED generator (2020-calibrated stylized facts ONLY) + the 2020 real OOS -- NOT real future data. (2) The longshort INSURANCE leg VIOLATES long-only+spot -> the gated book is RESEARCH; deploy needs the user's explicit LO-exception sign-off. (3) DETECTOR pre-registered on the 2020 TRAIN band ONLY then FROZEN -- never fit on OOS/the synthetic test paths. (4) The SHUFFLE control is a random-timed toggle of EQUAL on-frequency -- the detector must beat it to claim TIMING skill (not just exposure). (5) Short-borrow MODELLED at 20.0bps/yr (prorated per-bar). (6) maker cost, no MtM double-count, lag-1 causal. (7) >=20 seeds; distributions (mean +- spread + WORST seed). (8) The 2020-calibrated synthetic bear is SHORT (~12% of the stitched cycle) and mild in NET terms; the verified mechanism (Q3b) is that the gate's FALSE-ALARM cost in the 88% non-bear time outweighs the small bear gain -- so the NULL is specific to a SHORT/MILD bear. A DEEPER or LONGER bear (a larger NET share of the cycle, e.g. a 2022-style grind-down) is the regime where binary insurance could flip +EV; it is NOT testable on the 2020 band and is the explicit open door (the detector + book are BUILT and VALIDATED to run on such a band on request).
